mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment rate above 7.5% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold being breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals strong demand momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through corporate earnings optimism.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and RSAFS growth fails to produce positive returns in > 50% of cases, or their interaction term shows negative Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 100 indicates robust supply-side activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days via improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO growth, or the signal fails in > 40% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 1.5% suggests deflationary risks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as central bank policy tightening becomes imminent.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns remain positive in > 60% of cases when CPIAUCSL falls below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 200k combined with falling unemployment (UNRATE < 7.0%) signals a tight labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS/UNRATE signal fails to produce positive returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term shows negative Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 800k indicates weakening demand for construction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as sectoral risks materialize.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns remain positive in > 60% of cases when HOUST falls below 800k.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.0% signals a tightening cycle, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS above 4.0%, or the signal fails in > 40% of cases.
